displayName.description: CdoId: 1200; FieldId: 7992; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: DisplayName is the field generally used to represent an object in a user interface, especially when the user interface needs to represent an object with just one data element.  For example, to represent a revisioned object one would ordinarily need to show both the name and the revision of the object.   The display name will include enough necessary elements to represent the object--in the example of a revisioned object it will include both the name and the revision, with a separator character.   This is especially useful when viewing objects in combo boxes and other one-column lists, the display name should be used.  DisplayName is usually a non-persistent field.

notes.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9092; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Notes/Comments

onDefaultRoute.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9093; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: Specifies whether the Step is on the default route.  The default route starts at the first step (specified by the Workflow) and follows the default path from each Step.

optional.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 28727; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Specifies whether the step is optional and can be skipped

paths.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9094; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: List of all the Paths originating at this Step

paths-Expanded.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9094; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: List of all the Paths originating at this Step

pathSelectors.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9095; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Paths selectors are used to select a Path based on the value(s) of specific data within a transaction. Each Path selector includes an expression to evaluate and a Path to select. The expression to evaluate identifies a field (see note 1) to evaluate and a comparison value. The comparison value can be a single value, a range (from-through values), or a list of values. Expressions are evaluated in a defined sequence. The first expression that evaluates to true determines the Path to select.

pathSelectors-Expanded.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9095; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Paths selectors are used to select a Path based on the value(s) of specific data within a transaction. Each Path selector includes an expression to evaluate and a Path to select. The expression to evaluate identifies a field (see note 1) to evaluate and a comparison value. The comparison value can be a single value, a range (from-through values), or a list of values. Expressions are evaluated in a defined sequence. The first expression that evaluates to true determines the Path to select.

reworkPaths.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9373; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: ReworkPath

reworkPathSelectors-Expanded.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9377; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: ReworkPathSelector

sequence.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9098; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Relative sequence number

stepType.description:
CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9099; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Denotes whether this Step references a Step or a Workflow. The values are:
1 = Spec
2 = Workflow

wipMsgLabel.description: CdoId: 7098; FieldId: 9140; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Identifier for relating a WIP Message to one or more Steps. WIP Messages defined for all Modeling Entities except a Step or Operation can be qualified with a WIP Message Label. This allows the same message to be associated with multiple Steps.

For example, to represent a revisioned object one would ordinarily need to show both the name and the revision of the object.   The display name will include enough necessary elements to represent the object--in the example of a revisioned object it will include both the name and the revision, with a separator character.   This is especially useful when viewing objects in combo boxes and other one-column lists, the display name should be used.  DisplayName is usually a non-persistent field.

instanceID.description: CdoId: 3730; FieldId: 15194; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: Unique ID for an instance of this object, generated by the system.; OriginalType: BaseObject

durationPerUnit.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 7130; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the amount of time needed to process a single unit. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

isFrozen.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 8324; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: Determines if changes are allowed to this instance (or any of its revisions)

runRateOption.description:
CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3971; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This option determines if run rate is specified as hours per unit or units per hour or time per batch. The values and their meanings are:
1 = Hours per unit (RunRateTime)
2 = Unites per hour (RunRateQuantity)                                                                                                                                                       
3 = Time Per Batch (RunRate Time)

setupTime.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3973; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the time required for a Setup at this Operation. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

stdBatchSize.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3974; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the standard size of a batch of material processed using this Spec. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

timePerBatch.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 27388; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specified the time needed to process a single Batch.

unitsPerHour.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3972; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the number of units processed in an hour. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

unscheduledTime.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 27387; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Process time that is not contained in the UPH,HPU or Batch Time entries.

yield.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3975; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Specifies the standard yield expected for processing with this Spec. This value is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

fastCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 5240; FieldId: 7196; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is a calculated value that denotes the time required processing a standard size batch of material using this Spec, assuming fast queue time. This is used to determine, for example, how quickly a job can be expedited through the Factory.

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

forwardCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 5240; FieldId: 4485; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is the sum of all NormalCycleTime from this step through the end of the Workflow (following defaults Paths from this Step forward).

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

forwardYield.description:
CdoId: 5240; FieldId: 4486; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Standard (cumulative) yield from this step through the end of the Workflow (following defaults Paths from this Step forward).

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

normalCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 5240; FieldId: 7197; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is a calculated value that denotes the time required processing a standard size batch of material using this Spec, assuming normal queue time.

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

eS_ResolvedSteps.description:
CdoId: 7099; FieldId: 689968504; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: Steps are used to discretely define processing for a point (step) within a Workflow. A Workflow is a collection of Steps that are related using Paths. Each Step contains zero or more Paths, which link to another Step. One Path at each Step is identified as the default Path.

A sequence of Steps and Paths is a Route. A Workflow can include multiple Routes. Only one Route is identified as the Standard Route. The Standard Route is determined by starting with the Step that is identified as the first Step and then iterating forward selecting the default path at each step.

A Specification Step is a step within a Workflow that includes a reference to a Spec which in turn describes the work that is to be performed.

; OriginalType: SpecStepChanges

eS_ResolvedSteps-Expanded.description:
CdoId: 7099; FieldId: 689968504; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: Steps are used to discretely define processing for a point (step) within a Workflow. A Workflow is a collection of Steps that are related using Paths. Each Step contains zero or more Paths, which link to another Step. One Path at each Step is identified as the default Path.

A sequence of Steps and Paths is a Route. A Workflow can include multiple Routes. Only one Route is identified as the Standard Route. The Standard Route is determined by starting with the Step that is identified as the first Step and then iterating forward selecting the default path at each step.

A Specification Step is a step within a Workflow that includes a reference to a Spec which in turn describes the work that is to be performed.

; OriginalType: SpecStepChanges

totalCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 7099; FieldId: 9126; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is the sum of all through the Workflow (following defaults Paths).

Note: The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

totalYield.description:
CdoId: 7099; FieldId: 9127; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Standard (cumulative) yield through the Workflow (following defaults Paths).

Note: The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

Bearer.description:
Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. 

 Enter 'Bearer' and then your token in the text input below.

Example: "Bearer 12345abcdef"
Bearer.name: Authorization
